children.text: Not later than one year after

children.tail: , the Secretary of Commerce, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Defense, the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, the Secretary of the Department in which the Coast Guard is operating, the Regional Fishery Managment
So in original. Probably should be “Management”.

Councils, interested States, Interstate Fishery Commissions, and representatives of the private sector, shall develop and publish a long-term plan which will meet the purpose of this chapter and be consistent with the standards established under

children.text: geographic, hydrographic, geologic, biological, ecological, social, economic, and other criteria for siting artificial reefs;
design, material, and other criteria for constructing artificial reefs;
mechanisms and methodologies for monitoring the compliance of artificial reefs with the requirements of permits issued under
mechanisms and methodologies for managing the use of artificial reefs;
a synopsis of existing information on artificial reefs and needs for further research on artificial reef technology and management strategies; and
an evaluation of alternatives for facilitating the transfer of artificial reef construction materials to persons holding permits issued pursuant to

children.tail: , including, but not limited to, credits for environmental mitigation and modified tax obligations.

children.text: For transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of
